data.body:
PASS-1 review (T5c — Security overlay) on the kill-switch/disable-config + rollout-ordering follow-on plan: PASS / not-applicable, no findings.

The plan's three deliverables add no new security surface in rebar's domain: (1) a project-level fail-open config toggle to disable the gate (an application config preference, like the existing verify.* toggles — rebar.toml:5-21, src/rebar/config.py:260); (2) a written rollback/back-out + deploy-ordering runbook for the REVIEW_RESULT-sidecar/SIGNATURE event scheme (a documentation/process artifact over append-only event types); and (3) a spec cross-reference. None introduces a new endpoint, network exposure, authn/authz boundary, storage/transmission of sensitive data or PII, or a new credential/secret/grant. rebar is a local, git-backed CLI/library, so per T5c's explicit not-applicable clause for apps without such a surface, the overlay PASSES.

The signing-key secret lifecycle the referenced signature scheme depends on is PRE-EXISTING and already sound, and is REUSED rather than added by this plan: HMAC key resolved from REBAR_SIGNING_KEY or a per-env, gitignored, 0o600 `.signing-key` generated on first use (README.md:289-315, src/rebar/signing.py); client/LLM output exposes only the key fingerprint via key_id, never the raw HMAC (tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_signature.py:292-307). This plan does not touch key custody, add a grant, or introduce plaintext secrets — so T5c's secret-lifecycle / least-privilege / data-protection checks do not engage as NEW concerns. Any secret already in the repo/tickets is an upstream concern, not this review's (ANTI-FP).

Affirmations (T5c): not-applicable — no new endpoint/path or network exposure (no auth-mechanism check needed); no new authn/authz boundary; no new sensitive-data/PII storage or transmission (no at-rest/in-transit encryption surface added); no new credential/role/grant (no least-privilege/wildcard concern); no new plaintext-secret handling (existing HMAC key custody is env-var/gitignored-0600-file with fingerprint-only exposure, unchanged by this plan).
